name: Dunar de Matalascañas – running circular along the coast
name_original: Dunar de Matalascañas – running circular por la costa
region_intro: Jogging around Almonte offers diverse routes through a picturesque 19th-century mill town nestled along the Canadian Mississippi River. The region features scenic landscapes, including riverfront paths, conservation areas, and rail-to-trail conversions. Joggers can explore a combination of paved trails, boardwalks, and more rugged terrain, with the Mississippi River and its cascades forming a central natural feature. The area provides varied urban and natural landscapes, making it suitable for different running preferences. Best jogging routes around Almonte The most popular jogging route is the Raya Real and Paseo Marismeño Loop, a 7.7 miles (12.5 km) trail that takes 1 hour 30 minutes to complete. This route offers a moderate challenge with varied terrain. Another top favourite among local runners is the Dunar de Matalascañas – running circular along the coast, an easy 2.9 miles (4.6 km) path. This trail provides a gentle run with coastal views. Local runners also love the Cuesta Maneli Trail, a 3.0 miles (4.9 km) trail leading through natural landscapes, often completed in about 36 minutes. Jogging around Almonte is defined by riverfront paths, conservation areas, and rail-to-trail conversions. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy strolls to longer, more extensive runs. The routes in Almonte are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 3 reviews. More than 400 runners have used komoot to explore Almonte's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many jogging routes are available in Almonte?
A: Almonte offers a good selection of running routes, with over 20 trails mapped on komoot. These range from easy strolls to more extensive, moderate runs, ensuring options for various fitness levels.

Q: What types of terrain can I expect on Almonte's running trails?
A: Jogging routes in Almonte feature a mix of terrain. You'll find riverfront paths, sections of rail-to-trail conversions like the Ottawa Valley Recreational Trail, and more natural, rugged paths within conservation areas such as the Mill of Kintail. The Almonte Riverwalk, for instance, combines paved trails and boardwalks.

Q: Are there any easy, family-friendly running routes in Almonte?
A: Yes, Almonte has several easy running options suitable for families. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e984549101/dunar-de-matalascanas-running-circular-along-the-coast" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dunar de Matalascañas – running circular along the coast&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 4.6 km path offering gentle coastal views. The Almonte Riverwalk is also a very accessible 1.3 km trail with historical plaques and scenic river views, perfect for a relaxed family outing.

Q: Where can I find longer or more challenging running routes in Almonte?
A: For a longer, moderate challenge, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e984515190/raya-real-and-paseo-marismeno-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Raya Real and Paseo Marismeño Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which spans 12.5 km and takes about 1 hour 30 minutes to complete. The Ottawa Valley Recreational Trail also offers extensive sections for longer runs through varied landscapes.

Q: Are there any running routes in Almonte that feature waterfalls or scenic river views?
A: Absolutely! The Almonte Riverwalk is specifically designed to showcase the Canadian Mississippi River and its cascades, including the impressive Grand Falls. Jogging along this trail provides stunning views of the river and its natural features right in the heart of town.

Q: What do other runners say about the jogging experience in Almonte?
A: The running routes in Almonte are highly regarded by the komoot community, boasting an average rating of 5.0 stars from over 3 reviews. Runners often praise the picturesque scenery, the variety of trails, and the charming small-town atmosphere.

Q: Are there any dog-friendly running trails in Almonte?
A: Many of Almonte's trails, particularly those in conservation areas and multi-use paths like the Ottawa Valley Recreational Trail, are generally dog-friendly. However, it's always best to check local signage for specific leash requirements and restrictions in certain areas, especially within protected natural spaces.

Q: Can I find circular running routes in Almonte?
A: Yes, Almonte offers several circular running routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e984549101/dunar-de-matalascanas-running-circular-along-the-coast" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dunar de Matalascañas – running circular along the coast&lt;/a&gt; is an example of an easy circular path. Many trails, including sections of the Riverwalk and routes within conservation areas, can be combined to form loops.

Q: What are some interesting landmarks or natural features to see while running in Almonte?
A: While running in Almonte, you can explore several points of interest. The Almonte Riverwalk features historical plaques detailing the town's mill history and offers views of the Grand Falls. The Mill of Kintail Conservation Area includes an 1830s grist mill. You might also encounter geological displays at Metcalfe Geoheritage Park or enjoy birdwatching near the Almonte Lagoons. For nearby attractions, consider visiting the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/885349" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of El Rocío&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4091374" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Charco Del Acebrón&lt;/a&gt; lake.

Q: Are there any running routes with outdoor physical activity stations?
A: Yes, the Almonte Wellness Trail is specifically designed with an accessible outdoor space that includes various physical activity stations. It also features a 'Tree of Life Labyrinth' for meditative walks, making it a unique option for combining exercise with mindfulness.

Q: What is the best season for jogging in Almonte?
A: Almonte offers beautiful jogging opportunities year-round. Spring and fall provide pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery, with lush greenery in spring and colorful foliage in autumn. Summer is also popular, though it can be warmer. Winter jogging is possible, especially on well-maintained trails, but conditions may vary with snow and ice.

Q: Are there any running routes that pass through or near the town center?
A: Yes, both the Almonte Riverwalk and the Ottawa Valley Recreational Trail pass directly through or very close to downtown Almonte. This allows runners to easily access trails and enjoy the charming 19th-century mill town atmosphere, often with views of the Mississippi River.
tips:
The Torre de la Higuera, also known as the "Matalascañas Stone", is a beacon tower located on the beach of Matalascañas, in the municipality of Almonte, province of Huelva. This tower was built in 1577 during the reign of Philip II as part of a coastal defence system against attacks by corsairs and pirates.

The tower suffered significant damage during the Lisbon earthquake in 1755, causing it to tip over and remain in its current position. Today, it is an iconic symbol of the area and has been declared a Site of Cultural Interest since 1985.
This trail runs through the dune of La Higuera beach within the Doñana Natural Park, a beautiful route with a wonderful landscape to enjoy with the whole family.

waypoints.name: Views of the Torre de la Higuera
